raw:
{
  "weights": {
    "R1.1": 0.7,
    "R1.2": 0.7,
    "R1.3": 0.7,
    "R1.4": 0.7,
    "R1.5": 0.3,
    "R1.6": 0.7,
    "R1.7": 0.7,
    "RX.1": 1.0,
    "RX.2": 1.0,
    "RX.3": 1.0,
    "RX.4": 1.0,
    "RX.5": 1.0
  },
  "active_set": [
    "R1.1",
    "R1.2",
    "R1.3",
    "R1.4",
    "R1.6",
    "R1.7",
    "RX.1",
    "RX.2",
    "RX.3",
    "RX.4",
    "RX.5"
  ],
  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "Should the review prioritize (A) technical content and claims (conceptual framing, logic, novelty, citations), (B) language/clarity and section flow, or (C) both equally?",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "The review should restate and stress-test the section’s core thesis and contribution framing to ensure the gap and claim are clear.",
    "R1.2": "It should verify that the progression from background to proposed method and conclusions is logically coherent.",
    "R1.3": "It should check that performance and practicality claims are tied to the stated benchmarks and metrics as reported in the text.",
    "R1.4": "It should estimate the delta versus nearest prior graph-based and compression methods to gauge novelty.",
    "R1.6": "Improving sentence-level clarity, concision, and tone is a common expectation in a section review.",
    "R1.7": "The review should consider whether citations are accurate and whether key related work is sufficiently covered.",
    "RX.1": "The review should disclose limits on what can be assessed from this excerpt and flag assumptions.",
    "RX.2": "It should identify and temper any overstatements that exceed the support provided by the text.",
    "RX.3": "Feedback should stay within the scope of reviewing this section rather than redesigning experiments or the whole paper.",
    "RX.4": "The review should avoid recommendations that could mislead readers or misattribute prior work.",
    "RX.5": "Comments should reference specific sentences or claims to maintain traceability and provenance."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: Should the review prioritize (A) technical content and claims (conceptual framing, logic, novelty, citations), (B) language/clarity and section flow, or (C) both equally?

rationale.R1.1: The review should restate and stress-test the section’s core thesis and contribution framing to ensure the gap and claim are clear.
rationale.R1.2: It should verify that the progression from background to proposed method and conclusions is logically coherent.
rationale.R1.3: It should check that performance and practicality claims are tied to the stated benchmarks and metrics as reported in the text.
rationale.R1.4: It should estimate the delta versus nearest prior graph-based and compression methods to gauge novelty.
rationale.R1.6: Improving sentence-level clarity, concision, and tone is a common expectation in a section review.
rationale.R1.7: The review should consider whether citations are accurate and whether key related work is sufficiently covered.
rationale.RX.1: The review should disclose limits on what can be assessed from this excerpt and flag assumptions.
rationale.RX.2: It should identify and temper any overstatements that exceed the support provided by the text.
rationale.RX.3: Feedback should stay within the scope of reviewing this section rather than redesigning experiments or the whole paper.
rationale.RX.4: The review should avoid recommendations that could mislead readers or misattribute prior work.
rationale.RX.5: Comments should reference specific sentences or claims to maintain traceability and provenance.
